# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>
You are a developer migrating from Git to jj. You have a repository at `/home/user/repo`. There are two bookmarks, `feature-a` and `feature-b`, which modify the same line in `app.py` in conflicting ways. Your task is to:
1. Create a new merge commit with both `feature-a` and `feature-b` as parents.
2. Resolve the conflict in `app.py` so that it contains both features (e.g., both function calls). The final `app.py` should look like:
```python
def main():
    print("Feature A")
    print("Feature B")
```
3. Move the `feature-a` bookmark to this new resolved merge commit.

todos.content: Explore the repository and identify the state of feature-a and feature-b bookmarks
Create a merge commit with feature-a and feature-b as parents using jj
Resolve the conflict in app.py to include both features
Move the feature-a bookmark to the new merge commit

arguments.command: jj log -r "feature-a | feature-b" && jj bookmark list

results.content:
○  owwmnztx test@example.com 2026-03-06 18:55:36 feature-b 72abaa4b
│  (empty) Add Feature B
~

○  xwpqqwmp test@example.com 2026-03-06 18:55:34 feature-a 90105dbb
│  (empty) Add Feature A
~
feature-a: xwpqqwmp 90105dbb (empty) Add Feature A
feature-b: owwmnztx 72abaa4b (empty) Add Feature B
main: xkzvqozu 4c4df6fc Initial commit

todos.content: Explore the repository and identify the state of feature-a and feature-b bookmarks
Create a merge commit with feature-a and feature-b as parents using jj
Resolve the conflict in app.py to include both features
Move the feature-a bookmark to the new merge commit

results.content:
Working copy  (@) now at: pptrtmur 8e1429b0 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : xwpqqwmp 90105dbb feature-a | (empty) Add Feature A
Parent commit (@-)      : owwmnztx 72abaa4b feature-b | (empty) Add Feature B

todos.content: Explore the repository and identify the state of feature-a and feature-b bookmarks
Create a merge commit with feature-a and feature-b as parents using jj
Resolve the conflict in app.py to include both features
Move the feature-a bookmark to the new merge commit

arguments.command: jj files -r feature-a && jj files -r feature-b

results.content:
error: unrecognized subcommand 'files'

  tip: a similar subcommand exists: 'file'

Usage: jj [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

For more information, try '--help'.

Command exited with code 2

arguments.command: jj file list -r feature-a && jj file list -r feature-b
jj show -r feature-a && jj show -r feature-b

results.content:
Commit ID: 90105dbb20fad51967cae6f1ef4a2f614f9ee431
Change ID: xwpqqwmpuzplqmxpokuwpznqoxnmqpxp
Bookmarks: feature-a feature-a@git
Author   : Test User <test@example.com> (2026-03-06 18:55:34)
Committer: Test User <test@example.com> (2026-03-06 18:55:34)

    Add Feature A

Commit ID: 72abaa4b5968739dfb7fcb921689a9df403ba5f8
Change ID: owwmnztxpqmttxxtqspmkxuvmmnqskyz
Bookmarks: feature-b feature-b@git
Author   : Test User <test@example.com> (2026-03-06 18:55:36)
Committer: Test User <test@example.com> (2026-03-06 18:55:36)

    Add Feature B

arguments.command: jj diff -r feature-a-..feature-a && jj diff -r feature-b-..feature-b
jj cat app.py -r feature-a && jj cat app.py -r feature-b

results.content:
error: unrecognized subcommand 'cat'

Usage: jj [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

For more information, try '--help'.

Command exited with code 2

arguments.command: jj file show app.py -r feature-a && jj file show app.py -r feature-b
jj diff -r xkzvqozu..feature-a && jj diff -r xkzvqozu..feature-b

results.content:
The working copy has no changes.
Working copy  (@) : pptrtmur 8e1429b0 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-): xwpqqwmp 90105dbb feature-a | (empty) Add Feature A
Parent commit (@-): owwmnztx 72abaa4b feature-b | (empty) Add Feature B

arguments.command: jj file show app.py -r feature-a && echo "---" && jj file show app.py -r feature-b && echo "---" && jj file show app.py -r main
jj file list -r feature-a --recursive && jj file list -r feature-b --recursive

results.content:
error: unexpected argument '--recursive' found

  tip: to pass '--recursive' as a value, use '-- --recursive'

Usage: jj file list --revision <REVSET> [FILESETS]...

For more information, try '--help'.

Command exited with code 2

arguments.command: jj file list -r feature-a && jj file list -r feature-b && jj file list -r main
jj file show app.py -r feature-a | cat -A && jj file show app.py -r feature-b | cat -A && jj file show app.py -r main | cat -A
jj diff -r feature-a-..feature-a --git && jj diff -r feature-b-..feature-b --git

results.content:
Working copy  (@) now at: pptrtmur f36ee438 Merge feature-a and feature-b
Parent commit (@-)      : xwpqqwmp 90105dbb feature-a | (empty) Add Feature A
Parent commit (@-)      : owwmnztx 72abaa4b feature-b | (empty) Add Feature B
